_patch_meta.description: Session B completion patch for registry 160 (thought). Sets session_b_status to Session B Complete. Corrects the recurring applicator anomaly where update_registry in the PREANALYSIS patch left session_b_status as Pre-Analysis Complete rather than Analysis Complete. Session B analysis document produced: WA-160-thought-analysis-20260326.docx. 74 working terms analysed across 6 parts. 3 research flags active (PH2-160-001 HIGH extraction anomaly; PH2-160-002 phronesis vol. limit; PH2-160-003 logismos vol. limit). This is the third consecutive registry with the recurring applicator anomaly.

operations.description: RECURRING APPLICATOR ANOMALY — third consecutive registry (98 justice, 34 covenant, 160 thought). update_registry ops in PREANALYSIS patches are not executing: session_b_status remains Pre-Analysis Complete instead of Analysis Complete. patch_history shows patch_id=null and applied_at=null for all three registries. OP-001 in this patch sets registry 160 directly to Session B Complete. Claude Code must investigate and resolve the patch applicator engine before the next registry. The pattern is consistent: update_registry ops fail silently while update_mti_status ops succeed.

Session B analysis complete for registry 160 (thought). Document: WA-160-thought-analysis-20260326.docx (649 paragraphs, 6 parts). Working set: 74 terms (56 extracted, 18 extracted_thin). Pre-analysis conducted across two patches: V1 (167 ops, 164 deletes including 5 HFA DB_ONLY_PROTECTED) and V2 (66 ops, 63 NULL classifications, 2 vol. limit flags, registry update). Key finding: thought vocabulary is always morally directional in Scripture. Significant analytical cluster: chashav, machashavah, nous/phronema, renewal of mind. HIGH priority: PH2-160-001 extraction anomaly requires programme-wide audit.
